value: Users pre-authorize transaction amounts through two-factor authentication.

evidence.quote: the National Payments Corporation of India (NPCI), the federal body behind the country’s widely used Unified Payments Interface (UPI), partnered with OpenAI and fintech firm Razorpay to enable consumers to shop and pay directly through ChatGPT.
India has kicked off a pilot to let consumers shop and pay directly through AI chatbots
enabling payments to be completed directly within ChatGPT without switching to external apps or websites.
Razorpay confirmed to TechCrunch that the pilot is being rolled out nationwide and will become widely available in the coming months.

the National Payments Corporation of India (NPCI), the federal body behind the country’s widely used Unified Payments Interface (UPI), partnered with OpenAI and fintech firm Razorpay to enable consumers to shop and pay directly through ChatGPT. Razorpay confirmed to TechCrunch that the pilot is being rolled out nationwide and will become widely available in the coming months.
the new AI-led experience could help NPCI drive further adoption by embedding UPI into everyday commerce if consumers embrace chatbots as a new interface for shopping.
AI companies will not get access to the payment data under the new setup, and users will pre-authorize the amount transacted through chatbots through two-factor authentication.

The experience is built on UPI Reserve Pay — a new NPCI protocol that allows users to block a specific amount of funds for future debits to designated merchants — and UPI Circle , a solution that delegates UPI authentication, enabling payments to be completed directly within ChatGPT without switching to external apps or websites.

Mathur told TechCrunch that the company has also completed a proof-of-concept for the new agentic payment experience with Google’s Gemini and Anthropic’s Claude. These integrations will go live for consumers in a few weeks, he said.
